design_direction: Luxurious minimalism with warm earth tones — editorial fashion aesthetic. Single-page cinematic scroll experience. Differentiated from industry-standard black/white luxury sites through warm oat + charcoal + sienna palette and Playfair Display serif typography.

oat.usage: Primary background, nav backdrop, footer bg

oat-dark.usage: Border colors, subtle dividers, hover states

charcoal.usage: Primary text, dark section backgrounds, button bg

soft-black.usage: Darkest accents, contrast text on oat

warm-gray-light.usage: Input borders, placeholder text, subtle overlays

warm-gray-dark.usage: Hover text, active nav states, caption text

sienna.usage: Decorative blur elements, subtle highlights, About section accent

display.usage: Brand name, section headings, large display text

body.usage: Body text, navigation labels, form inputs, captions

widest.usage: Nav links, category labels, form labels

section-padding.usage: Reusable .section-padding class for horizontal content padding

section-vertical.usage: Vertical padding for major sections (Collections, Lookbook, About, Contact)

components.name: Nav
components.purpose: Fixed navigation header with smooth scroll links, transparent-to-frosted-glass transition on scroll, mobile hamburger menu

props.scrolled: boolean — whether page has scrolled past 60px threshold

interactions: Scroll listener triggers bg-oat/90 + backdrop-blur-md transition at 60px
Hamburger icon animates to X on mobile menu open (rotate + translate)
Mobile menu overlay with staggered link animations
Smooth scroll to section anchors on nav click

components.name: Hero
components.purpose: Full-viewport hero with parallax editorial image, brand name, tagline, scroll indicator

props.scrollY: number — current scroll position for parallax calculation
props.progress: number — scroll progress (0-1) for fade/scale calculations

interactions: Parallax: background image moves at 2x scroll rate, scales up slightly
Content fades out as user scrolls (opacity based on scrollY / viewportHeight)
Staggered entrance animations: subtitle → brand name → tagline → scroll indicator
Scroll indicator bounces infinitely

components.name: Collections
components.purpose: Three collection cards displayed in responsive grid with hover interactions

props.collections: Collection[] — array of collection objects with id, name, title, description, image

interactions: Card lifts with Y translation on hover (whileHover: { y: -8 })
Image zooms slightly on hover (scale 1.08)
Description text expands below card on hover (AnimatePresence height animation)
Staggered reveal animations on scroll into view

components.name: Lookbook
components.purpose: Masonry-style image grid with hover captions and full-screen lightbox viewer

props.images: Image[] — array of image objects with src and caption

interactions: Image grid with hover caption overlay (opacity transition)
Click opens full-screen lightbox with scale animation
Lightbox closes on backdrop click or close button
Image caption displayed below lightbox image

components.name: About
components.purpose: Brand philosophy section with editorial image, three-paragraph origin story, and stats grid

props.stats: Stats[] — array of stat objects with value and label

interactions: Two-column layout: image left, text right (stacks on mobile)
Decorative sienna blur element in background
Staggered reveal animations for image, label, heading, paragraphs, stats
Stats grid with border-top divider

components.name: Contact
components.purpose: Contact section with brand info, email addresses, Paris address, and contact form

props.formData: { name: string; email: string; message: string } — form state
props.submitted: boolean — whether form has been submitted

interactions: Form with underline-style inputs (border-b only, no box)
Submit shows animated 'Thank you' confirmation
Form resets after 3 seconds
Button with hover fill animation (bg slides up from below)

components.name: Footer
components.purpose: Site footer with brand name, tagline, nav links, copyright
interactions: Static footer with border-top divider
Nav links repeat for easy access
Dynamic copyright year

components.name: AnimatedSection
components.purpose: Reusable scroll-reveal wrapper — fades up sections as they enter viewport
interactions: Uses Framer Motion useInView hook (once: true, margin: '-80px')
Initial state: opacity 0, translateY 48px
Animated state: opacity 1, translateY 0
Transition: duration 0.9s, ease cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)

sections.notes: Fixed position, spans full width, z-50
h-screen, parallax image, brand name, tagline, scroll indicator
bg-oat, py-32 md:py-44, 3-column grid, editorial cards
bg-oat, py-32 md:py-44, masonry grid, lightbox
bg-charcoal text-oat, py-32 md:py-48, two-column layout, stats grid
bg-oat, py-32 md:py-44, two-column layout (info + form)
bg-oat, border-top, nav links repeat, copyright

seo_recommendations: Add Open Graph meta tags (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url) — Entono currently has basic metadata only
Add Twitter Card meta tags (twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description) — standard for fashion brand social sharing
Add JSON-LD structured data (Brand schema) — helps search engines understand Entono as a fashion brand
Add favicon/brand icon — Entono may not have one yet
Consider adding robots.txt with standard allow rules
performance_recommendations: Image optimization critical — Entono's 7 images total ~10MB. Convert to WebP/AVIF format, add loading='lazy' for below-fold images
Consider using Next.js Image component for automatic optimization, or manual preprocessing with sharp/ImageMagick
Preload hero image for fastest LCP — add <link rel='preload'> in layout.tsx
Framer Motion animations are GPU-accelerated (transform + opacity only) — Entono uses them correctly
Consider font-display: swap (already set) — Entono uses next/font/google correctly
